action.hypothesis: `lowrank_two_stage_residual` has now had its K128 signum recipe mostly clarified, and the operator allows exactly one matched architecture probe. The highest-value single probe is `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual`: it preserves the same low-rank staged static-library decomposition while changing only the support-selection mechanism. If routing cannot beat or closely pressure the current `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor under the exact same `K=128, EF=12, signum, LR=2.0e-3` recipe, then this extra routing complexity is not worth continuing in the current LUTurbo-aligned regime.
action.summary: Run one matched architecture probe by switching only `ARCHITECTURE` to `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` while keeping the current mainline recipe fixed at `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=2.0e-3`, and the baseline loss/runtime settings unchanged. Judge the probe by the whole curve, not just endpoint FVU: if routing stays smooth and materially challenges the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` anchor, it remains worth later reserve consideration; if it is clearly weaker or less stable, close the family-worthiness question and return to the `lowrank_two_stage_residual` mainline next round.
action.self_review: This obeys the matched-probe rule: one probe only, one primary variable only, and `K`, `OPTIMIZER`, `LR`, and `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12` stay fixed. I chose `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` because it is the cleanest structural contrast to the current mainline without abandoning the same low-rank staged export contract. The risk is that routing adds online selection complexity, so even a small FVU gain may not justify follow-up; this round is explicitly a worthiness screen, not a promotion commitment.
notes_to_memory: Round 84 uses the single allowed matched architecture probe on `routed_lowrank_two_stage_residual` against the exact `lowrank_two_stage_residual` K128/EF12/signum/LR=2.0e-3 anchor.
